seoTitle: Lockleys vs Long Plains — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lockleys and Long Plains. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lockleys vs Long Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lockleys and Long Plains.
heading: Lockleys vs Long Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lockleys, South Australia and Long Plains, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lockleys has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Long Plains (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lockleys or Long Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lockleys has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Long Plains scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lockleys scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Long Plains scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lockleys or Long Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lockleys has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
